name: Lactate sensor (LldR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects L-lactate, a fermentation and clinical metabolite. The LldR lactate-responsive regulator gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the food-grade GRAS chassis Lactococcus lactis.

input.detects: L-lactate in fermentation, food, or clinical samples
input.operatingRange: LldR-based sensors respond across physiological lactate (low mM)

sensing.sensorMechanism: LldR de-represses / activates its target promoter (Plld) in response to L-lactate.
sensing.signalFlow: L-lactate -> LldR activates Plld -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a weak reporter promoter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with lactate.

parts.name: LldR regulator
parts.notes: Lactate-responsive regulator; requires porting/codon-optimisation for L. lactis.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed upstream of a weak reporter promoter

parts.name: dCas9-ω activator
parts.source: CRISPRa (Bikard et al. 2013); CRISPRi demonstrated in lactic acid bacteria

performance.limitOfDetection: LldR module: low-mM lactate (module-validated in E. coli).
performance.notes: LldR lactate sensing and CRISPRa are validated separately; the integrated device in L. lactis is a design template and the sensor module needs porting to the GRAS host.

safety.biocontainment: thyA-based biological containment demonstrated in L. lactis; food-grade host.
safety.notes: GRAS / food-grade chassis (no LPS endotoxin); suited to food-fermentation monitoring and ingestible research applications.

buildSteps.title: Port + design
buildSteps.description: Codon-optimise LldR/Plld for L. lactis; design the CRISPRa sgRNA.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: LldR. TU2: Plld -> sgRNA. TU3: dCas9-omega. TU4: weak promoter -> mCherry. Use NICE-compatible vectors.

buildSteps.title: Expose to lactate
buildSteps.description: Validate across a lactate standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining an LldR lactate-sensing module with L. lactis CRISPRa
partsSources: Aguilera et al. 2008 / E. coli lldPRD (LldR lactate sensing)
Bikard et al. 2013, NAR (CRISPRa); CRISPRi in lactic acid bacteria
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
